Conclusion. The Resurrection of the Flesh in Its Absolute Identity and Perfection. Belief of This Had Become Weak. Hopes for Its Refreshing Restoration Under the Influences of the Paraclete.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: Conclusion. The Resurrection of the Flesh in Its Absolute Identity and Perfection. Belief of This Had Become Weak. Hopes for Its Refreshing Restoration Under the Influences of the Paraclete.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: And so the flesh shall rise again, wholly in every man, in its own identity, in its absolute integrity.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Wherever it may be, it is in safe keeping in God’s presence, through that most faithful “Mediator between God and man, (the man) Jesus Christ,” who shall reconcile both God to man, and man to God; the spirit to the flesh, and the flesh to the spirit.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Both natures has He already united in His own self; He has fitted them together as bride and bridegroom in the reciprocal bond of wedded life.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Now, if any should insist on making the soul the bride, then the flesh will follow the soul as her dowry.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: The soul shall never be an outcast, to be had home by the bridegroom bare and naked.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: She has her dower, her outfit, her fortune in the flesh, which shall accompany her with the love and fidelity of a foster-sister.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But suppose the flesh to be the bride, then in Christ Jesus she has in the contract of His blood received His Spirit as her spouse.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Now, what you take to be her extinction, you may be sure is only her temporary retirement.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: It is not the soul only which withdraws from view.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: The flesh, too, has her departures for a while—in waters, in fires, in birds, in beasts; she may seem to be dissolved into these, but she is only poured into them, as into vessels.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: And should the vessels themselves afterwards fail to hold her, escaping from even these, and returning to her mother earth, she is absorbed once more, as it were, by its secret embraces, ultimately to stand forth to view, like Adam when summoned to hear from his Lord and Creator the words, “Behold, the man is become as one of us!”—thoroughly “knowing” by that time “the evil” which she had escaped, “and the good” which she has acquired.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Why, then, O soul, should you envy the flesh?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: There is none, after the Lord, whom you should love so dearly; none more like a brother to you, which is even born along with yourself in God.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: You ought rather to have been by your prayers obtaining resurrection for her: her sins, whatever they were, were owing to you.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: However, it is no wonder if you hate her; for you have repudiated her Creator.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: You have accustomed yourself either to deny or change her existence even in Christ—corrupting the very Word of God Himself, who became flesh, either by mutilating or misinterpreting the Scripture, and introducing, above all, apocryphal mysteries and blasphemous fables.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But yet Almighty God, in His most gracious providence, by “pouring out of His Spirit in these last days, upon all flesh, upon His servants and on His handmaidens,” has checked these impostures of unbelief and perverseness, reanimated men’s faltering faith in the resurrection of the flesh, and cleared from all obscurity and equivocation the ancient Scriptures (of both God’s Testaments) by the clear light of their (sacred) words and meanings.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Now, since it was “needful that there should be heresies, in order that they which are approved might be made manifest;” since, however, these heresies would be unable to put on a bold front without some countenance from the Scriptures, it therefore is plain enough that the ancient Holy Writ has furnished them with sundry materials for their evil doctrine, which very materials indeed (so distorted) are refutable from the same Scriptures.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: It was fit and proper, therefore, that the Holy Ghost should no longer withhold the effusions of His gracious light upon these inspired writings, in order that they might be able to disseminate the seeds of truth with no admixture of heretical subtleties, and pluck out from it their tares.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: He has accordingly now dispersed all the perplexities of the past, and their self-chosen allegories and parables, by the open and perspicuous explanation of the entire mystery, through the new prophecy, which descends in copious streams from the Paraclete.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: If you will only draw water from His fountains, you will never thirst for other doctrine: no feverish craving after subtle questions will again consume you; but by drinking in evermore the resurrection of the flesh, you will be satisfied with the refreshing draughts.
